children.heading: General prohibition
children.label: a General prohibition
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, no Federal disaster relief assistance made available in a flood disaster area may be used to make a payment (including any loan assistance payment) to a person for repair, replacement, or restoration for damage to any personal, residential, or commercial property if that person at any time has received flood disaster assistance that was conditional on the person first having obtained flood insurance under applicable Federal law and subsequently having failed to obtain and maintain flood insurance as required under applicable Federal law on such property.

children.label: A
children.text: obtain flood insurance in accordance with applicable Federal law with respect to such property, if the property is not so insured as of the date on which the property is transferred; and

children.label: B
children.text: maintain flood insurance in accordance with applicable Federal law with respect to such property.

children.label: A
children.text: the transferee fails to obtain or maintain flood insurance in accordance with applicable Federal law with respect to the property,

children.label: B
children.text: the property is damaged by a flood disaster, and

children.label: C
children.text: Federal disaster relief assistance is provided for the repair, replacement, or restoration of the property as a result of such damage,

children.heading: Property described
children.label: 3 Property described
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1), a property is described in this paragraph if it is personal, commercial, or residential property for which Federal disaster relief assistance made available in a flood disaster area has been provided, prior to the date on which the property is transferred, for repair, replacement, or restoration of the property, if such assistance was conditioned upon obtaining flood insurance in accordance with applicable Federal law with respect to such property.
the Secretary of Agriculture finds, or has found, to have been substantially affected by a natural disaster in the United States pursuant to ; orsection 1961(a) of title 7

the President declares, or has declared, the existence of a major disaster or emergency pursuant to the Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act ( et seq.), as a result of flood conditions existing in or affecting that area.42 U.S.C. 5121

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: e Effective date
children.text: This section and the amendments made by this section shall apply to disasters declared after .September 23, 1994
